architecture_fit.note: model_fn is exact softmax(QKᵀ/√d_head)·V on CUDA; reproduces reference to machine precision (mse ~1e-15).

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform-attention strawman (baseline_mse) is measured and plotted vs model MSE; relies on the built-in baseline rather than adding extra strawmen.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic exact mechanism so it is tautologically faithful, but no ablation/causal check and no README acknowledging the synthetic nature.

operating_range.note: Holds at cos≈1.0 across seq_len 8→128 (>1 order of magnitude); fidelity is flat with no edge degradation.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully written out by hand (analytical attention, nothing learned), exactly reproducing the target.

visual_judgement.note: Rich Demo tabs: sweep metric plots, attention heatmaps, pred-vs-gt scatter, per-head table; slightly busy but legible and defaults to latest run.

visualisation_rationale.note: No README.md at all, so the required 'Why this visualisation' justification is entirely absent.

notes: A textbook-correct implementation of scaled dot-product attention on the GPU that reaches near-machine-precision fidelity (attention_fidelity ≈ 1.0, cos_sim ≈ 1.0) across the entire seq_len 8→128 sweep, well above the uniform-attention baseline. The Gradio app is thorough and legible. The main gap is the complete absence of a README.md, so there is no documented method delta from base_model.py, no faithfulness/ablation discussion, and no visualisation rationale — which caps the otherwise-perfect result at borderline.
